'''Jay Pitts''' (born 9 December 1989) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} or {{rlp|lf}} for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]].<ref name="Statistics at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

He previously played for the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]], [[Leeds Rhinos]], [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]] and the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Super League]], and the [[London Broncos]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] and the [[Super League XXIV|top flight]].

==Background==
Pitts was born in [[Ossett]], [[West Yorkshire]], England.

==Playing career==
He has also played at club level for the [[Bradford Bulls]], [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]], [[Leeds Rhinos]] and [[Hull F.C.]]{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

Pitts signed a contract with [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] beginning at the end of the 2009 season.

In March 2012, Pitts signed a {{frac|2|1|2}}-year deal with [[Hull F.C.]] after they negotiated a deal with [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]].{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

[[File:Jay Pitts.jpg|thumb|right|Pitts playing for the Bradford Bulls]]

==Bradford Bulls==
'''2014''' - [[2014 Bradford Bulls season|2014 Season]]

Pitts featured in round 11 against [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] to round 15 [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]]. Pitts then played in round 17 against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] to round 27 [[London Broncos]]. In the [[Challenge Cup]] he featured in round 5 [[Catalans Dragons]] to the Quarter Final against Warrington.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

'''2015''' - [[2015 Bradford Bulls season|2015 Season]]

Pitts signed for Bradford on a permanent basis on a two-year deal. He featured in the pre-season friendlies against [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] and [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]].

He featured in round 1 [[Leigh Centurions]] to round 23 [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]]. Pitts played in Qualifier 1 [[Sheffield Eagles|Sheffield]] to Qualifier 7 Halifax R.L.F.C. Pitts played in the £1 Million Game against Wakefield Trinity. He also featured in the [[Challenge Cup]] in round 4 [[Workington Town]] to round 5 Hull Kingston Rovers.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

'''2016''' - [[2016 Bradford Bulls season|2016 Season]]

Pitts featured in the pre-season friendlies against [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] and [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]].

He featured in round 1 [[Featherstone Rovers]] to round 21 r[[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]]r then in round 23 against Featherstone. Pitts played in the Championship Shield Game 1 against Whitehaven to the final against Sheffield. Pitts played in the [[Challenge Cup]] in the 4th round against [[Dewsbury Rams|Dewsbury]].{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

==London Broncos==

Pitts signed a two-year deal with London following Bradford's liquidation.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12212/10552455/london-broncos-sign-jay-pitts-from-bradford-bulls|title=London Broncos sign Jay Pitts from Bradford Bulls|work=Sky Sports|date=26 August 2016|access-date=20 February 2017}}</ref>

'''2017''' - [[London Broncos|2017 Season]]

Pitts featured in round 1 [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]] to round 7 [[Rochdale Hornets|Rochdale]]. He also played in the Challenge Cup in round 4 against the [[Toronto Wolfpack]].

==Wakefield Trinity==
In 2020, Pitts joined [[Wakefield Trinity]] from the [[London Broncos]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/wakefield-trinity-star-jay-pitts-ready-make-use-big-game-experience-2533616/|title=Wakefield Trinity star Jay Pitts ready to make use of big-game experience|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk}}</ref>
Pitts played 27 games for Wakefield Trinity in the [[Super League XXVIII]] season as the club finished bottom of the table and were relegated to the [[RFL Championship]] which ended their 24-year stay in the top flight.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/20876/12962152/super-league-wakefield-trinity-relegated-after-loss-at-leigh-leopards-st-helens-and-wigan-warriors-win/|title=Super League: Wakefield Trinity relegated after loss at Leigh Leopards, St Helens and Wigan Warriors win|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>
